All assertions pass in both configurations -- the quality assertions (covers-both-libraries, explains-v4-config-change, etc.) are non-discriminating between with-skill and without-skill for these topics, since the model's training data is recent enough to cover them well.
With-skill runs take ~2.4x longer (118s vs 49s avg) and use ~1.9x more tokens (20k vs 11k avg) due to ctx7 CLI lookups.
Eval 1 (multi-library-lookup) exceeded the skill's 3-command limit by running 5 commands -- the 'nextjs' query didn't find /vercel/next.js on the first try, requiring a retry with 'next.js'.
The skill's value proposition is harder to demonstrate on topics well-covered in training data. Tests with newer/niche libraries would better showcase the documentation freshness advantage.
Process assertions (ran-ctx7-library, ran-ctx7-docs) confirm the skill workflow is being followed correctly in 2 of 3 evals (eval 1 needed a retry).
